title: acokanthera oppositifolia
intro: Acokanthera oppositifolia, also known as the poison arrow plant or khatikati, is a plant species in the Apocynaceae family, native to tropical Africa. The plant contains toxic compounds called cardenolides, which possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Acokanthera oppositifolia include its potential use in treating fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions, as well as its ability to act as an antiseptic and anti-parasitic agent. Acokanthera oppositifolia is often used in traditional medicine, and herbal preparations, such as infusions and decoctions, are made from the leaves, roots, and bark of the plant.
